subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: we wish to argue that the sorts of social considerations that we discuss here are crucial to understanding the persistence and spread of virtually all false beliefs
rationale: Core thesis: collective belief is corrupted by social selection on credibility and conformity, not by lossy transmission.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: by exerting influence on how legitimate, independent scientific results are shared with the public, the would-be propagandist can substantially affect the public's beliefs about scientific facts
rationale: The sharing gate is the steerable surface: manipulating selection shifts belief without touching the evidence.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: the mere introduction of misinformation cannot explain its widespread acceptance
rationale: Injecting content is insufficient; a parallel social-selection mechanism explains which falsehoods take hold.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: those who wished to generate uncertainty could take advantage of the ways beliefs spread in a social network, and the human tendencies that regulate this process
rationale: Names the social dynamics of belief propagation as the exploitable surface attackers co-opt.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: industry can influence science without biasing scientists themselves by engaging in what they call "industrial selection"
rationale: Industrial selection injects industry-favorable results into the pipeline without fraud or biased scientists.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: even perfectly rational---albeit simple---agents who learn from others in their social network can fail to form true beliefs about the world, even when more than adequate evidence is available
rationale: False consensus arises from network structure among rational agents, not from any complexity property of the idea.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: individually rational agents can form groups that are not rational at all
rationale: Group-level false belief is a network-structure effect, not produced by lossy re-encoding at hops.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: scientists tend to place greater trust in colleagues who have reached the same conclusions they have reached, and less in those who hold radically different beliefs
rationale: The belief-update gate's selection criterion is source agreement, a fitness measure uncorrelated with truth.

subject: concept:echo-chambers-vs-bubbles
quote: Social commentators have long noted the emergence of online "echo chambers" in political discourse; models of social learning allow us to say why these changes matter so much
rationale: Supplies formal network-model machinery for why echo-chamber-like structures form and resist outside evidence.

subject: concept:manipulation-surface
quote: this tendency to conform can be weaponized---as evidenced by Russian interventions in the US and UK in 2016
rationale: Develops conformity and trust dynamics as concrete, weaponizable attack surfaces with empirical instantiation.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: it is amplified by algorithms that social media sites use to identify which stories are highly engaging so as to maximize their viewership
rationale: Engagement-tuned algorithmic gates systematically amplify fake high-arousal content over calibrated reporting.

subject: concept:algorithm-as-selection-engine
quote: It was, after all, a Facebook algorithm that actively promoted a fake story, hosted on ETF News, about Megyn Kelly
rationale: Treats the recommendation algorithm as the operative tunable selection gate, not neutral transport.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: simply amplifying a subset of the evidence by sharing it more broadly can lead a community of policy makers, or the public, to become confident in a false belief, even as scientists themselves converge to the true belief
rationale: Selective amplification inverts truth value: same true evidence, public lands false because of what got picked.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: Notice that in this model, the propagandist does not fabricate any data. They are performing real science... They just publish the results selectively.
rationale: Damage is done purely at the sharing gate with no transport loss or fabrication: selection is the operative mechanism.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: When journalists share what they take to be most interesting---or of greatest interest to their readers---they can bias what the public sees in ways that ultimately mislead, even if they report only on real events
rationale: Journalism is the bridge-zone gate where selection pressure (novelty bias) actively distorts specialist evidence.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: this is precisely what happens when journalists focus on novel, surprising, or contrarian stories---the sorts that are most likely to gain attention, arouse controversy, and be widely read or shared
rationale: The journalist-gate picks the highest-fitness finding, not the most representative one.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: Once scientists start to share evidence... it becomes extremely likely that they will all come to believe the same thing, for better or worse
rationale: Denser networks converge faster on consensus, true or false; the effect is convergence, not a complexity ceiling.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: the propagandist will be most effective if they run and publicize the most studies with as few data points as possible
rationale: Fragmenting evidence multiplies spurious variants for a selection gate to pick; selection is the lever optimized.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: the effects of propaganda can occur even in the absence of a propagandist. If journalists make efforts to be "fair"... they can bias what results the public sees in deeply misleading ways
rationale: Neutral gate criteria (fairness, novelty) corrupt belief on their own, with no malicious actor needed.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: Whatever barriers we erect against the forces of propaganda will immediately become targets for these sources to overcome... an asymmetric arms race
rationale: Institutional defenses are decided by ongoing capture-resistance against an adaptive adversary.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: Wikipedia has developed a commendable standard for writing about controversial scientific topics
rationale: Endorses Wikipedia's proper-weighting editorial apparatus as a curation layer surfacing consensus-weighted evidence.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: organizations like Facebook, Twitter, and Google are responsible for the rampant spread of fake news on their platforms... and, ultimately, for the political, economic, and human costs that resulted
rationale: Locates accountability for engagement-optimized selection with the platforms that own and tune the infrastructure.

subject: concept:epistemic-vigilance
quote: trusting the judgments of others does not always work so neatly when these judgments are not actually independent of each other
rationale: Weighting evidence by source trust is a real cognitive defense that misfires under non-independence.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: some of the most obvious interventions to reduce the spread of propaganda and fake news, such as breaking down barriers that prevent the spread of reliable information between different communities, are unlikely to succeed
rationale: Challenges the naive integration prescription: connecting distrustful networks fails and can worsen polarization.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: in a group where not everyone listens to everyone else, pockets of scientists can be protected from misleading data
rationale: Zollman effect: less connectivity can improve group belief, an edge case where reduced coupling helps.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: industrial groups can exert pressure on the community of scientists to produce more results favorable to industry... by increasing the amount of work produced by well-intentioned scientists who happen to be wrong
rationale: Funding makes truth-losing methodologies reproduce by training more students: selection as a natural-selection analogue.

subject: source:misinformation-age
quote: how we change our beliefs in light of evidence depends on the reputation of the evidence's source. The propagandist's message is most effective when it comes from voices we think we can trust.
rationale: Source reputation is itself an attack surface; weaponizing trust steers belief without touching evidence.

_note: Catalog gaps the book develops: rational agents reaching false consensus (its central result); polarization-via-distrust and conformity-bias as mechanisms; weaponized-reputation; the Zollman effect. Biased-production and selective-sharing mapped to selection here.
